rationale: Hebrew 'Moriah' (2 OT occurrences: Gen 22 Aqedah + 2 Chr 3 Temple Mount) anchors a canonical typology — Aqedah's 'only/beloved son' on Moriah → Father's voice over Christ on the same range; both units explicitly cite Gen 22.
Greek θριαμβεύω (only 2 NT occurrences, both cited by both seeds as the fulfillment) explicitly binds Ark's procession and Samson's victory-in-death to Christ's triumph over powers (Col 2:15).
Σιών is the load-bearing geographic-theological term on both sides: Zion as the King's mount in Zech 9 / Matt 21 and as the Lamb's eschatological mountain in Rev 14 — a deliberate canonical inclusio.
ἱλασμός occurs only twice in the NT, both in 1 John (2:2 and 4:10); the link is not an echo but the author's own deliberate cross-reference framing Christ's propitiation.
Νῶε is the central proper noun on both sides; 1 Pet 3:20-21 and Heb 11:7 explicitly tie the Noahic-covenant flood to baptism into Christ — a textbook canonical typology.

ὀστέον links Joseph's preserved bones (Heb 11:22) with the unbroken bones of the Passover Lamb (John 19:36); the typology of preserved-bones-as-resurrection-anticipation is real but rarely made explicit.

ὁμοιότης (only Heb 4:15, 7:15) is the technical word for Christ as priest 'after the likeness of' Melchizedek and tempted in our likeness — directly contrasting Levi's office with the new High Priest.
Νῶε is the central name on both seeds; 1 Pet 3:20-21 binds Noah's salvation through water to baptism into Christ — one of the most canonically affirmed typologies.
ὁμοιότης (Heb 4:15, 7:15) is central to the Levitical-priest seed (likeness/contrast with Christ) but only tangential to the breastplate; the lexical bridge is thin on one side.
ἁμάρτημα anchors Paul's mercy-seat language (Rom 3:25) and Yom Kippur sin-removal; the term carries the sacrificial-cleansing theme on both sides.
ἁλληλουϊά (4 NT occurrences, all in Rev 19) directly anchors the eschatological wedding-feast climax; both seeds explicitly converge on Rev 19's marriage-supper Hallelujah.
ὡραῖος ('beautiful') connects Nahum's herald-feet (cited Rom 10:15) with Acts 3's Beautiful Gate; Luke's wordplay is real but the typological depth is modest.
Jericho is the shared geographic stage where Luke deliberately frames both mercy-narratives (Luke 10/18); the city's typological weight as the cursed entry-point of conquest enriches the echo.
ἱλασμός (only 1 John 2:2 and 4:10) is the precise propitiation-vocabulary tying Yom Kippur's mercy-seat to Christ as our advocate; a primary canonical echo.

ἀμνός explicitly invokes Christ as the Lamb (John 1:29, 1 Pet 1:19) and the Servant 'led as a lamb' (Acts 8:32 quoting Isa 53); Yom Kippur's victim-goat and Isaiah's suffering-lamb converge in the New Testament's lamb-Christology.
μεσίτης is doing principal theological work in Heb 12:24 (where Christ is the new-covenant mediator) and answers Job's cry for a daysman — the lexical and conceptual bridge is direct.
ἦχος knits Sinai's 'sound of words' (Heb 12:19) to Pentecost's 'sound from heaven' (Acts 2:2); a deliberate canonical Sinai↔Pentecost echo that Luke and Hebrews exploit.
ἀμνός (Lamb) binds the Passover firstborn-deliverance lamb to Isaiah's suffering Servant-lamb; 1 Pet 1:19 explicitly fuses both into Christ's blood.
אֵיבָה (enmity, Gen 3:15) is the woman-and-seed serpent-warfare term; 'Woman, behold your son' echoes Gen 3:15's 'the woman' but enmity itself is not foregrounded in the cross-saying.

These two units are essentially the same passage (Ezek 36 new-heart promise) seen from seed and prophecy angles; λουτρόν (Eph 5:26, Tit 3:5) is the NT word that retrieves Ezekiel's sprinkling and washing.
λέων ties Judah's lion-blessing (Gen 49) to Daniel's first beast and to Rev 5:5's Lion-of-Judah; the link runs through Revelation more than between the two source units directly.

Ἰεσσαί appears in Matt 1's genealogy that also names Rahab (Matt 1:5-6) and Paul cites 'root of Jesse' in Rom 15:12 — Rahab's scarlet cord typology and Isaiah's branch are joined through Davidic-messianic lineage.

ἐγκαινίζω occurs only at Heb 9:18 and 10:20 — the latter explicitly identifies Christ's flesh as the inaugurated veil; the lexical bridge between the new-covenant mediator and the torn veil is direct and canonical.
κρύσταλλος (only Rev 4:6 and 22:1) is John's deliberate fusion-word: crystal sea before Ezekiel's throne and crystal river of Zech/Ezek's living waters share one Apocalypse vocabulary.
ἀμνός anchors Passover-firstborn's lamb in the broader blood-covenant of Christ (1 Pet 1:19); the lamb's blood is the canonical hinge.
ἐξαγοράζω anchors Gal 3:13 — 'Christ redeemed us from the curse of the law, becoming a curse by hanging on a tree' — directly fusing the curse-seed and tree-seed.
τράγος (4 NT occurrences, all in Heb 9-10) is the Yom Kippur goat that Hebrews repeatedly contrasts with Christ's once-for-all blood; lexical link is precise and theologically central.
The lexeme node 'hilasmos' is by construction the word's home; 1 John 2:2 is one of its two NT occurrences and the seed's theological center.
Same ὀστέον bridge: Joseph's preserved bones (Heb 11:22 — by faith) and Passover-lamb unbroken bones (John 19:36) — a real but second-tier echo of preservation-through-death.
Ἰεσσαί directly binds David-the-youngest-anointed to Isaiah 11's branch from Jesse's stump (cited Rom 15:12); the messianic lineage is the central typology.
Heb 10:20 (ἐγκαινίζω) makes the torn veil at Christ's death the inaugurated 'new and living way' opened by the new-covenant mediator — a direct lexical bridge.
συντρίβω (John 19:36 'not broken') is the Passover-bone preservation word, slightly adjacent to Zech 12's ἐκκεντέω piercing-word; the two units share the same Johannine passion-fulfillment cluster.
ἱλασμός (1 John 2:2, 4:10) directly translates the mercy-seat propitiation of Lev 16 into Christology — a primary canonical lexical bridge.
ἐκκεντέω (only 2 NT occurrences) is the word that fuses Dan 7's Son-of-Man-on-clouds with Zech 12's pierced-one in Rev 1:7 and John 19:37; explicit canonical fusion.
Rev 1:7's deliberate fusion of Zech 12 'they pierced' (ἐκκεντέω) with Dan 7 'coming on clouds' directly binds the pierced-side seed to the coming-on-clouds theophany.
σείω/shake is the precise word Heb 12:26 retrieves from Hag 2:6 to fuse Haggai's promised shaking with the cosmic-signs Day-of-the-Lord.
συμπληρόω (Acts 2:1 — 'when Pentecost was being fulfilled') unites two facets of the same Pentecost event; the word itself is shared by description and theophany of the same moment.
λέων binds Daniel's literal lions to Rev 13's beast 'whose mouth was like a lion's' (echoing Dan 7); the connection is real but channeled through Dan 7 rather than directly between these two units.
אֲפֵלָה (deep gloom) is the prophetic Day-of-LORD darkness vocabulary that Joel 2:2 and Zeph 1:15 share verbatim; a canonical inner-prophetic echo.

συντάσσω is Matthew's fulfillment-formula word ('as the Lord commanded'), used at both the Triumphal Entry (21:6) and Judas's silver (27:10) — both Zechariah-fulfillments framed by the same Matthean lexical signal.

διάδημα appears only in Revelation (3x); the link to Zech 6's priest-king crown runs through Christ's many-crowned-king-of-kings (Rev 19:12), but the antichrist's crowns are a counter-image, making the echo more contrastive than typological.

ἐναντίον is the discourse-marker 'before'; it appears in both the Emmaus disciples' summary (Luke 24:19) and the Ethiopian's Isa 53 quotation (Acts 8:32), but the word itself isn't doing typological work.

Aramaic אֲלַף (Dan 5:1, 7:10 — 'a thousand') is the Belshazzar/Ancient-of-Days word; the link to Felix's trembling-at-judgment runs through Dan 5's writing-on-the-wall pattern but the lexeme itself is peripheral.
σείω links Matt 27:51's earth-shaking at the veil's tearing with the eschatological cosmic-shake (Heb 12:26, Rev 6:13); the veil-seed is touched by the shake-motif but not centrally about it.

δούλη's three NT occurrences (Mary's Magnificat plus Acts 2:18 citing Joel) link Spirit-poured-out-on-handmaids with the Lukan annunciation pattern, but the Samson-birth seed is not primarily a handmaid-text — link is thin.

διασκορπίζω in Luke 15:13 (the prodigal scattering his wealth) is the same word as Zech 13:7 / Mark 14:27 (sheep scattered when the Shepherd is struck), but the senses are unrelated — surface echo only.
πρωτότοκος is Christ-title in Col 1:15,18 and Heb 1:6, and Heb 12:23 calls the church 'the assembly of the firstborn'; the lemma is central to both seed's theological core.
ἀμνός unites the Passover firstborn-deliverance lamb and the Yom Kippur substitute-goat under one NT lamb-of-God reading (1 Pet 1:19).

κιθάρα is central to Rev 14:2 (harpists with the Lamb on Zion) and Rev 5:8 (harp-bowls before the Lamb), but the Daniel-sealed-book seed isn't primarily a harp-text — link is via Revelation's broader scroll-Lamb context.
Acts 28's Malta viper-bite is Luke's deliberate Gen 3:15 enactment — Paul, like the woman's seed, suffers no harm from the serpent; אֵיבָה (enmity) is the typological key.
זֵעָה occurs only at Gen 3:19 (Adam's curse: 'by the sweat of your brow'); Luke 22:44's bloody sweat reverses Eden's sweat-curse in Gethsemane — Eden-Gethsemane typology is canonical.
υἱοθεσία is Paul's signature word for adoption (Rom 8:15,23; Eph 1:5; Gal 4:5); both seeds rest on this term as the structural pillar of pre-temporal election and Spirit-witness sonship.

ἐξαγοράζω (Gal 3:13 'redeemed... by the tree') is the bridge: Marah's bitter water sweetened by wood prefigures the redeeming tree-of-cross; lemma central on the tree-side, illustrative on the Marah side.

ἐνίστημι ('things present', Pauline) is essentially a temporal-adverb common in Paul; its appearance in 2 Thess 2:2 (the antichrist text) and absence from the Daniel 3 seed makes the link superficial.
ἁμάρτημα ties Yom Kippur's sin-removal to the mercy-seat propitiation of Rom 3:25; near-tautologous pairing of two facets of the same atonement-day.
ἐφάπαξ is the once-for-all word that Romans 6:10 and Hebrews 7-10 share to articulate the singular efficacy of Christ's death; both seeds rest on this lemma.
ἐφάπαξ in 1 Cor 15:6 means 'all at once' (500 witnesses simultaneously), distinct from its 'once for all' usage in Rom 6:10 — same lemma, different sense, modest typological weight.
μέτωπον (8 occurrences, all in Revelation) is John's seal-on-the-forehead vocabulary that bridges Rev 7's sealed 144,000 with Rev 22:4's eschatological face-to-face — a structural Revelation echo.
ἀπάτη (deceit, 2 Thess 2:10's lawless one) isn't a Joshua-typology word; the Joshua-Yeshua seed is about entering rest, not deceit — link is incidental.
ὄξος is the Ps 69:21 cross-drink shared by all four Passion accounts; both seeds are about the same moment and the same fulfilled-Psalm word.
ἐκπνέω is shared by Mark 15:37, 15:39 and Luke 23:46 — the Centurion's confession and Jesus's last words frame the same death-moment with the same lemma.
πλευρά (John 19:34 pierced + 20:20,25,27 risen-Jesus showing his side to Thomas) is a deliberate Johannine inclusio — Thomas's faith verifies the spear-mark of the pierced side.
δέκατος ('tenth') has no real role in the twelve-stones breastplate seed; Rev 11:13's 'tenth of the city' is unrelated — the lemma is coincidence.
πρωτότοκος names both Rom 8:29's firstborn-among-brothers and Heb 12:23's assembly-of-firstborn — the same Christological-ecclesial term grounding both seeds.
νεανίας ('young man') is a generic noun shared by Acts 7:58 (Saul) and Acts 20:9 (Eutychus); Luke uses it for narrative parallel but typological depth is minimal.
χρίω (Luke 4:18, Acts 4:27, 10:38, Heb 1:9) is the messianic anointing-word; the lexeme node and the Ps 2 anointed-king seed are deliberately tied through the Nazareth synagogue-reading.
ἀναπηδάω is a Lukan action-verb shared by Acts 14:14 and 16:29; a stylistic echo with minimal typological content.
ἐπισυναγωγή (only 2 Thess 2:1 and Heb 10:25) is the eschatological-gathering word; 2 Thess 2:1's gathering precedes the antichrist's revelation — a real assembly-typology link.
ἀμνός fuses the Yom Kippur sin-bearing animal with Isaiah's Lamb-Servant in 1 Pet 1:19 and Acts 8:32; the scapegoat-and-Servant share the Lamb-of-God reading.
צֶמַח (the Branch) is THE messianic title shared by Jer 23:5, Jer 33:15, Zech 3:8, Zech 6:12 — a canonical prophetic chain explicitly fused in second-Temple-period 'Branch' messianism.
ἱεράτευμα (only 1 Pet 2:5,9 — 'royal priesthood') bridges the Levitical set-apart-priests and the twelve-memorial-stones via 1 Pet's living-stones imagery, but the connection is indirect.
ῥομφαία is Revelation's eschatological-sword vocabulary (6:8, 19:15) tying the four-horsemen and Armageddon under one weapon-image.
σάκκος appears in Rev 6:12 (sun darkened) and 11:3 (witnesses clothed) — same Revelation-vocabulary but doing different work in each context.
κιθάρα (Rev 5:8, 14:2, 15:2) is the Lamb-throne harp-vocabulary shared across Rev 5, 14, 15 — a deliberate Revelation inclusio.
ἀνάμνησις is the institution-word ('do this in remembrance', Luke 22:19 / 1 Cor 11:24-25); both seeds rest on this lemma as the Eucharistic memorial.
ἀνάμνησις is the Eucharistic memorial-word that both seed and festival rest on — Luke 22:19 / 1 Cor 11:24-25 ground the Lord's Supper in Christ's institution.
κιβωτός (Heb 9:4 — Noah's ark and the ark-of-covenant holding Aaron's rod) is the same vessel-of-preservation word linking the two salvific containers.
εἴσειμι ('enter') is shared by Acts 3:3 (Peter entering at prayer-hour) and Heb 9:6 (priest entering tabernacle); Luke may be intending a priesthood-typology but the lemma is a routine motion-verb.

Hebrew חֲטָאָה ('a sin') is the OT sin-vocabulary undergirding both Hebrews-10's 'sacrifices cannot take away sins' and 1 John 2's propitiation — the OT lemma is foundational but not direct lexical link.

לוּט links Elijah wrapping his face at the still-small voice (1 Kgs 19:13) with Isaiah's veil that covers the peoples (Isa 25:7); both 'wrappings' converge in the eschatological-unveiling on the mountain.
κλείς binds Rev 1:18 ('keys of death and Hades') to Rev 20:1's 'key to the abyss'; the Risen Christ's authority over death anchors the millennial-reign vision.
ὁσάκις ('as often as') is a frequency-adverb shared by 1 Cor 11:25 ('as often as you eat') and Rev 11:6, but the two senses are unrelated — surface lemma match.
עֵדָה ('witness') is the covenant-memorial-stone word shared by Gen 31:52 (Jacob-Laban) and Josh 24:27 (Shechem); both covenants are explicitly framed with witness-stones.
συντρίβω is John 19:36's word for the unbroken bones of the Passover-lamb fulfillment, sharing the same Johannine passion cluster as Zech 12's pierced-one.
λουτρόν (Eph 5:26, Tit 3:5) is the Bride-washing word that retrieves Ezek 36's clean-water sprinkling — the Bride's new heart is the new-covenant fulfillment.
ἐκδέχομαι is a common 'wait/await' verb; its use in Heb 10:13 (post-atonement waiting) and Acts 17:16 (Paul's waiting) shares no real typology.
The lexeme node 'hilasmos' is the home-vocabulary for ἁμάρτημα/propitiation; mercy-seat is its OT antecedent — a structural lexical link.
φλόξ binds Acts 7:30's burning-bush retelling with Rev 1:14's flame-eyed Christ — both fire-theophanies of the Name and the risen Lord.
ἐκκεντέω applies only to the pierced-side side; the Joseph-reveals-self typology lives on different vocabulary, making this a weak lemma-only bridge.
κρύσταλλος (Rev 4:6, 22:1) is the John-of-Patmos vocabulary that fuses Ezek 1's throne and Ezek 47's river into one Apocalyptic landscape.
גְּזֵרָה occurs once at Lev 16:22 (the scapegoat's 'cut-off' land); the nasa-lexeme node explicitly explores this scapegoat-vocabulary as bearing-away.
ὁμοιότης is central to the Levi-Melchizedek priesthood contrast in Hebrews; breastplate is tangential — lemma central on one side only.
ἦχος unites the Sinai trumpet-sound (Heb 12:19) with Pentecost's 'sound from heaven' (Acts 2:2); Babel-Sinai-Pentecost is a recognized canonical chain.
ἐλαφρός ('light burden' / 'light affliction') is the gentle-yoke word; its appearance in two NT texts is unrelated to the Sabbath-covenant or 4 Ezra seed beyond surface.
דְּמָמָה appears at 1 Kgs 19:12 (still small voice) AND Ps 107:29 ('he made the storm a whisper that calmed the waves') — Psalm 107 directly bridges Elijah's whisper to storm-stilling.
σάκκος (sackcloth) is shared by Rev 6:12 and Rev 11:3 but the moon-to-blood and time-times-half-time prophecies don't intersect on this term.
ἐρήμωσις is the 'abomination of desolation' word that Matt 24:15 / Mark 13:14 explicitly draw from Dan 9:27 — a deliberate canonical citation.
ἔκτρωμα occurs once (1 Cor 15:8) — Paul's self-description as 'untimely born' at his Damascus-resurrection-appearance directly fuses both theophanies.
ἀντίτυπος is the typology-vocabulary itself (1 Pet 3:21's baptism-as-antitype-of-flood, Heb 9:24's earthly-copy-of-heavenly); the lemma binds these as theological method, not direct echo.
κόπτω ('mourn/wail') is the Zech 12:10 vocabulary that Matt 24:30 retrieves; the seed and prophecy of Zech 9's donkey-king share Matthew's deliberate fulfillment-citation.
κρύσταλλος fuses Ezek 1's throne-vision with Rev 22's river through Rev 4:6 and 22:1 — same Apocalyptic vocabulary anchoring both Edenic-temple visions.
ὁμοιότης (Heb 7:15 — priest 'after the likeness of Melchizedek') is the technical word for Hebrews' new-priesthood-typology binding the Levi-covenant to the office of priest.
ἐκζητέω links Acts 15:17's Amos-9 citation (Gentiles seeking the Lord) to Heb 11:6's Enoch ('God rewards those who seek him'); a real seek-the-LORD canonical motif.
ἦχος ties Sinai's terrifying trumpet-sound (Heb 12:19) to glory-returning theophanic-sound traditions, of which Ezek 43:2 is a primary instance ('sound of many waters').
ἄμεμπτος is doing real work in Heb 8:7 (the first covenant was not 'faultless') but is incidental to Daniel 12's wise-shining seed; lemma central on one side only.
ἐλαφρός has no thematic role in either Job's restoration or Sabbath-covenant; surface lemma coincidence.
τρέφω ('feed/nourish') is a common verb; its appearance in Rev 12:14 (woman nourished in wilderness) doesn't typologically connect to the Spirit-anointing of Isa 61.

μηρός occurs once at Rev 19:16 (the name 'King of Kings' on Christ's thigh); this directly connects the office-of-King with the prophetic Day-of-Lord judgment, though the Obadiah connection is mediated.
κτίσμα ('creature') appears in Rev 5:13 (worshipping creatures) and Rev 8:9 (dying sea-creatures); same Revelation-vocabulary but in opposite valences.
κιθάρα (Rev 5:8 — harps-with-golden-bowls-of-incense, which are the prayers of saints) directly fuses incense-altar and scroll-opening Lamb-vision; the lemma is genuine but mediated through Rev 5.
δουλεία binds Heb 2:15 (delivered from death-bondage) with Rom 8:15,21 (Spirit-not-of-bondage, creation freed from corruption-bondage); the lemma articulates the same liberation-Christology.
παράβασις (Rom 5:14 — Adam's transgression — and Gal 3:19 — law added because of transgressions, with the mediator) directly binds Adamic-fall to the need for a covenant-mediator.
ὁμοίωμα binds Phil 2:7's 'in likeness of men' to Rom 6:5's 'in likeness of his death' — both name the participatory-likeness Christology of incarnation and baptism.
Σαλμών (Matt 1:4-5) is Boaz's father in the Davidic genealogy; the two seeds share the same lineage-passage, making the lemma a structural anchor.
ἐξαλείφω runs through Rev 3:5 (not blotted out), 7:17 and 21:4 (tears wiped away) — the same erase/wipe vocabulary connecting white-garments-saints and great-multitude.
θνητός is the Pauline mortality-word (Rom 8:11, 1 Cor 15:53-54) deliberately fused with Isa 25:8's swallowed-up-death prophecy; the resurrection-of-mortal-bodies retrieves Isaiah's banquet.
ἐφάπαξ binds Rom 6:10 (Christ died once-for-all) with Heb 9:12 (entered the holy place once-for-all by his blood); Yom Kippur's repeated entry replaced by a single act.
הֲדֹם is the Ps 110:1 footstool of the enthroned Son (cited at Acts 2:35 in Peter's sermon and implicit at Stephen's right-hand vision); both Acts speeches retrieve the same Psalm.
διανοίγω is shared by Luke 24:31,32,45 (Emmaus opened-eyes-Scriptures) and Acts 7:56 (heavens opened at Stephen's vision); the same Lukan opening-vocabulary links Stephen-as-Christlike-martyr.
ταπείνωσις is Mary's word (Luke 1:48 — God regarded her humiliation) and James 1:9-10's lowly-exalted reversal; the Magnificat-humiliation pattern grounds James's wisdom-paradox.
ἔντρομος appears in Acts 7:32 (Moses trembling at the bush) AND Heb 12:21 (Moses on Sinai 'I tremble with fear') — explicit theophany-fear vocabulary spanning bush and Sinai.
προέρχομαι ('went on ahead') is a common motion-verb; appearances in Matt 26:39 (Gethsemane) and Luke 1:17 (John as forerunner) are coincidental rather than typological.
מִפְתָּן ('threshold') is the temple-threshold word (Ezek 9-10 glory departing); its connection to the pierced-side blood-and-water seed is thematic at best.
συνανάκειμαι is a Synoptic table-fellowship verb; its appearance in NT meals doesn't typologically connect Mephibosheth's table-fellowship with Nathan's confrontation beyond surface.
ὑετός links Acts 14:17 (Paul preaching the rain-giving God) with James 5:17-18 (Elijah's prayer for rain) — both connect divine-rain with apostolic-prophetic prayer.
μεσονύκτιον (midnight) marks Acts 16:25 (jailer's hymn-prayer) and Acts 20:7 (Eutychus context) — Luke's recurring midnight-deliverance motif anchors both.
ὑάλινος (only Rev 4:6 and 15:2) is the sea-of-glass throne-vocabulary that fuses Ezekiel's throne-vision with the Lamb's-victors scene — same Apocalyptic vision-furniture.
συντελέω is shared by Heb 8:8 (citing Jer 31 'I will complete a new covenant') and Luke 4 (Jesus's wilderness ended); same lemma but doing different work in each unit.
κλείς (Rev 1:18, 3:7, 9:1, 20:1) is the Revelation-keys vocabulary — open-door of David (3:7) and key-to-abyss (9:1) share the same authority-of-Christ frame.
πεντακόσιοι ('five hundred') is shared by Luke 7:41 (parable's debt) and 1 Cor 15:6 (witnesses); pure numerical coincidence with no typological link.
Hebrew קְרָב ('battle') appears in OT war-Psalms; the Acts 8 Philip-Ethiopian Isa-53 seed isn't a battle text, and Rev 19's battle is in different vocabulary.
πικραίνω is shared by Rev 8:11 (waters made bitter) and Rev 10:9-10 (scroll made stomach bitter) — same Revelation-bitter motif of judgment-tasting.
συλλέγω (Matt 13:28-30,40-41) is the harvest/separation verb shared by Wheat-Tares and Dragnet — two adjacent Matt-13 kingdom parables with the same eschatological-gathering lemma.
אֵיבָה (Gen 3:15 enmity) is fulfilled in Acts 28 where Paul shakes off the serpent unharmed; the serpent-seed-of-woman warfare directly explains Malta.
ῥαντίζω is the Hebrews-9-10 sprinkling-vocabulary that ties red-heifer ashes (Num 19) and tabernacle-veil consecration to Christ's blood — central on both sides.
ὑπόδειγμα is central to Heb 8:5's tabernacle-as-heavenly-pattern but not to Jordan-crossing; central on one side only.
κόπτω (mourn) is shared by Matt 24:30's 'all tribes wail' (citing Zech 12:10) and the Triumphal Entry's Zech-9-fulfilment cluster — both retrieve Zechariah.
ἥμισυς binds Rev 11:9,11 (half-week of dead witnesses) with Rev 12:14's 'half a time' — Revelation's persistent 3.5-period motif.
παράδεισος (only 3 NT occurrences) fuses Joseph's prison-promise-of-life with Christ's Paradise-promise (Luke 23:43) and Rev 2:7's tree-of-life-in-Paradise — a real Eden-restoration thread.
ὁρίζω ('appointed/determined') is a generic NT verb; no real thematic connection between Josiah's reforms and Sabbath-covenant via this lemma.
צֶמַח (the Branch) explicitly appears in both Zech 3:8 and Zech 6:12 — the same Zechariah Branch-prophecy on the same Joshua-priest figure; near-identity.
ἔριφος ('young goat') links Luke 15:29's elder-brother complaint with Matt 25:32's sheep-vs-goats judgment; a real but secondary parabolic echo of inclusion-vs-exclusion.
סְנֶה occurs only in the Sinai-bush passages (Exod 3:2-4, Deut 33:16); Acts 2's fire that rests but does not consume retrieves the same bush-fire pattern explicitly named in both seeds.
ἔσω ('within/inside') is a common locative; its presence in Rom 7:22 ('inner man') and Mark 14:54 (Peter inside the courtyard) is unrelated.
שָׁפָט is Elisha's patronymic (1 Kgs 19:16,19; 2 Kgs 3:11); the lemma names the very Elisha to whom Elijah's mantle passes — direct narrative continuity.
δίστομος ('two-edged') has no real link to Josiah's reforms; the lemma is central to Heb 4:12 and Rev 1:16 but tangential to either side here.
Hebrew שָׁוָה appears in Ps 16:8 (cited Acts 2:25) but is not load-bearing for the Vine-seed; lemma significant on the Peter-Joel side only.
δειπνέω (Rev 3:20 'I will sup with him' + 1 Cor 11:25 new-covenant cup) ties Wisdom's open-table invitation to the new-covenant Lord's Supper — a real wisdom-Eucharist motif.
Ἰεριχώ is the same locale where Joshua-Yeshua brought down walls and the new Yeshua opened blind eyes (Mark 10:46); deliberate canonical-locale echo.
διαπεράω is a generic 'crossed over' verb; its use across Synoptic boat-crossings and Luke 16:26 (gulf-not-crossable) are unrelated.
אֲלַף (Dan 7:10's 'thousand thousands') is the Ancient-of-Days myriad-attendant vocabulary that Rev 4-5 retrieves; both throne-visions share Daniel's court.
אֲלַף (Dan 7:10) is the throne-court myriads-language Rev 4 and Final-Judgment scenes both retrieve from Dan 7.
πολίτης (Luke 15:15 + 19:14) is Luke's hostile-citizen word — both parables use it for the exile-among-Gentiles motif; a Lukan stylistic-typological echo.
δούλη (handmaid) is the Joel-2:29 / Acts-2:18 Spirit-on-handmaids word, exactly Mary's self-designation in Luke 1:38,48 — Luke deliberately fuses Mary as eschatological-handmaid of Joel.
Aramaic קַבֵּל is the same Daniel-Aramaic 'receive' verb (Dan 2:6, 6:1, 7:18 — saints receive kingdom); binds Daniel 6 (Daniel received) and Daniel 7 (saints receive kingdom).
ἀναπηδάω (Mark 10:50 Bartimaeus springing up + Acts 14:10 Lystra cripple) shows Luke patterning Pauline-healings on Jesus's healings — a real narrative typology.
πού ('somewhere' — Heb 2:6 and 4:4) is Hebrews' citation-formula word for both Ps 8 (Son-of-Man) and Gen 2 (Sabbath); a stylistic Hebrews-pattern marker.
πρέπω (Matt 3:15 'it is fitting to fulfill all righteousness') anchors both baptismal-voice and theophany units — the same event from two angles.
ἁμάρτημα anchors the lexeme-node's propitiation theology; Gen 3:21's first sacrifice (coats of skin) inaugurates the sin-covering pattern the lexeme explores.
בַּר (Aramaic 'son' — Dan 3:25 'son of the gods', Dan 7:13 son-of-man) is the Aramaic-son-of-Daniel vocabulary that the Ascension fulfills (Acts 1's Dan-7 enthronement).
ἥμισυς binds Rev 11:9,11's three-and-a-half days to Rev 12:14's 'half a time' — the same Danielic-half-period vocabulary that frames Revelation's tribulation.
μέτωπον (Rev 14:1, 14:9, 20:4) is John's forehead-mark-of-Lamb-vs-beast vocabulary spanning Lamb-on-Zion and the millennial reign — structural Revelation thread.
ἱματισμός links Luke 9:29's dazzling-Transfiguration-garments with John 19:24's stripped-and-divided crucifixion-garments (citing Ps 22) — a glory-vs-shame garment inclusio.
ὀργίζω (Matt 18:34, 22:7) is shared by Unforgiving-Servant and the Wedding-Banquet judgment — Matthew's same king-master-anger vocabulary in two judgment-parables.
διασκορπίζω (Luke 16:1 squandered + Matt 25:24 'gathering where you did not scatter') binds two stewardship-parables on the same scatter-vs-gather lemma.
γεμίζω (fill) is a common verb shared by Mark 4:37's swamping boat and John 2:7's filled water-jars; surface coincidence without typological depth.
Aramaic שִׁתִּין ('sixty') appears in both Dan 3:1 (image) and Dan 6:1 (satraps); same Daniel-Aramaic stylistic register but no typological force.
λέων ties the Lion-of-Judah Shiloh-prophecy (Gen 49:9-10) to Daniel-among-the-lions and ultimately to Rev 5:5's Lion-of-Judah-as-slain-Lamb.
ἀναγκάζω ('compel') is a common verb; usage in Matt 14:22 (disciples to boat) and Luke 14:23 (banquet invitation) is incidental.
πνέω ('blow') is shared by John 3:8 (Spirit blowing) and other wind-texts; doesn't directly connect Ezek 36's new heart with Rev 7's seal.
δίστομος ('two-edged sword') is central to Heb 4:12 and Rev 1:16's Ancient-of-Days-imagery but tangential to Josiah's reforms; lemma central on one side only.
ὡραῖος bridges Nahum/Isaiah 'beautiful feet' (cited Rom 10:15) and Acts 3's 'Beautiful Gate' as Luke's deliberate herald-of-good-news wordplay.
ὅσιος ('Holy One') is Ps 16:10's word cited at Acts 2:27 and 13:34-35 — the resurrection-foundation Peter and Paul both apply to Christ as forever-priest.
Hebrew טָלֶה (Isa 40:11 — 'he will gather the lambs in his arms') is the shepherd-lamb word; Rev 21's Lamb-throne consummates the Isa 40 shepherd-promise.
טָלֶה appears at Isa 40:11 and Isa 65:25 — the same Isaiah-lamb vocabulary spanning the Voice-Crying gospel-prologue and the wolf-and-lamb new-creation peace.
χρίω anchors Luke 4:18's Nazareth sermon (citing Isa 61's anointed) which announces the jubilee-sabbatical 'acceptable year'; the Anointed-King opens sabbatical fulfillment.
κλαυθμός is Matthew's signature 'weeping and gnashing of teeth' refrain (8:12, 13:42,50, 22:13) — both Dragnet and Wedding-Banquet share Matthew's eschatological-exclusion formula.
περιβλέπω is a Markan stylistic 'looked around' verb (Mark 3:5,34; 5:32; 9:8); usage doesn't bind the bleeding-woman and Transfiguration typologically.
εἰ δὲ μή ('lest/otherwise') is a generic Greek conditional; no typological connection between the two parables via this particle.
